theory.intro: Geometry uses agreed names and marks so a diagram can be described precisely. The order of letters matters for rays and angles.
facts: A **point** is named by one capital letter, such as point $A$.
An **interval** joins two endpoints; interval $AB$ and interval $BA$ name the same object.
A **ray** starts at its first named point and continues through the second, so ray $AB$ and ray $BA$ are usually different.
A **line** extends forever in both directions; line $AB$ and line $BA$ name the same line.
For $\angle ABC$, the middle letter $B$ is the vertex and the arms are rays $BA$ and $BC$.
Matching ticks mark equal intervals; matching arcs mark equal angles.

foundation.tikz: \begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.8]\coordinate (A) at (0,0);\coordinate (B) at (2,0);\draw[-{Stealth}] (A)--(3,0);\fill (A) circle (2pt) node[below] {$A$};\fill (B) circle (2pt) node[below] {$B$};\end{tikzpicture}

foundation.q: What do the matching single ticks show?
foundation.a: Interval $AB$ is equal in length to interval $BC$.

development.q: Identify the object and name it.
Name the ray that starts at $S$ and passes through $T$.
Give both valid three-letter names for the marked angle.
A student names the marked angle $\angle ACB$. Explain the error and give a correct name.
development.a: The vertex $B$ must be the middle letter, so a correct name is $\angle ABC$.

development.q: State all the equality information shown by the marks.
development.tikz: \begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.7]\coordinate (A) at (0,0);\coordinate (B) at (3,-2);\coordinate (C) at (3,2);\coordinate (D) at (3,0);\draw (A)--(B)--(C)--cycle;\draw (A)--(D);\foreach \p/\pos in {A/left,B/below,C/above,D/right}{\fill (\p) circle (2pt) node[\pos] {$\p$};}\draw (2.87,-1)--(3.13,-1);\draw (2.87,1)--(3.13,1);\draw (0.65,0) arc[start angle=0,end angle=34,radius=0.65];\draw (0.9,-0.62) arc[start angle=-34,end angle=0,radius=1.1];\end{tikzpicture}

mastery.q: A line contains points $P$, $Q$ and $R$ in that order. Name the ray that is the same as ray $PQ$ using $P$ and $R$.
The marked angle has vertex $O$ and arms through $A$ and $C$. Point $B$ lies on arm $OA$. Give four valid three-letter names for the angle.
mastery.tikz: \begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.75]\coordinate (O) at (0,0);\coordinate (B) at (1.3,0);\coordinate (A) at (3,0);\coordinate (C) at (1.4,2.2);\draw (A)--(O)--(C);\foreach \p/\pos in {O/below,A/right,B/below,C/above}{\fill (\p) circle (2pt) node[\pos] {$\p$};}\draw (0.65,0) arc[start angle=0,end angle=58,radius=0.65];\end{tikzpicture}
